notes: Global search over customers + debts. Per product truth (conventions.md AI guardrail) search is TRADITIONAL software, never AI. As-built the screen does NOT hit a dedicated search endpoint: it subscribes to repo.watchCustomers() (List<CustomerView>) + repo.watchDebts() (List<DebtView>) and filters entirely client-side in the StreamBuilder (search_screen.dart lines 82-118). Query is lowercased; a digits-only variant (q.replaceAll(RegExp('\\D'),'')) is matched against phone and amount/remaining. Customers match on name+phone (a customer with no debts is still findable); debts match on customer.name, customer.phone, debt.note, debt.amount, remaining. Results are two rendered subsets, not the full entities. No dedicated backend search endpoint is invented here; the backend may keep serving these two list reads and let the client filter, OR add a search query param later — reconciliation decision, not extracted here.

fields.name: id
fields.note: customer.id; nav target only. UUIDv7 target per S-2 (int autoincrement as-built).

fields.name: name
fields.note: customer.name; matched (contains, case-insensitive) + rendered as title + avatar seed.

fields.name: phone
fields.note: customer.phone (defaults ''); matched on digits-only variant; rendered via formatPhone().

fields.name: owed
fields.note: CustomerView.owed aggregate outstanding; rendered as amount (formatNaira). Kobo on the wire per S-1.

filtersOrSort: client-side filter: name contains(query) OR (digits present AND phone contains(digits))
no sort applied on this screen (stream order preserved)

reads.sourceCall: repo.watchDebts() -> List<DebtView> (DebtView = debt + joined customer + paidAmount)

fields.name: id
fields.note: debt.id; nav target only. UUIDv7 target per S-2.

fields.name: amount
fields.note: debt.amount; matched via debt.amount.toString().contains(digits). Kobo per S-1.

fields.name: remaining
fields.note: DebtView.remaining = (amount - paidAmount).clamp(0,amount); matched via remaining.toString().contains(digits); rendered as the row amount. Derived, not a stored column. Kobo per S-1.

fields.name: note
fields.note: debt.note; matched (contains, case-insensitive) + rendered in subtitle ('note · dueLabel').

fields.name: dueDate
fields.note: debt.dueDate; rendered as dueLabel() in subtitle (not matched).

fields.name: customer.name
fields.note: joined Customer.name; matched (contains) + rendered as title.

fields.name: customer.phone
fields.note: joined Customer.phone; matched on digits-only variant.

filtersOrSort: client-side filter: customer.name contains(query) OR (digits present AND (customer.phone contains(digits) OR amount.toString contains(digits) OR remaining.toString contains(digits))) OR note contains(query)

recentSearchHistory.behavior: In-memory session history (newest first), mutated at runtime: _remember() inserts on a successful search (dedup, cap 6, min length 2, lines 31-42); user can delete a row (_recents.removeAt, line 236). Shown as the idle/empty-query state (_buildRecents, lines 189-249). No persistence, no repository, no drift table.
recentSearchHistory.reconciliation: Likely stays a LOCAL DEVICE concern (like a keyboard MRU) — not a backend resource. Flagged for reconciliation; no endpoint extracted.

authContext.note: Reads customers + debts, both readable by owner and staff per conventions.md roles model. No owner-only capability on this screen.

special.note: Uses drift streams locally (offline-first), but search itself is deterministic traditional-software client-side filtering — no realtime backend requirement introduced by this screen.

uiStatesHandled: no-results: _NoMatches empty state ('No matches for “<query>”', when both customerHits and hits are empty)
results: grouped Customers and/or Debts cards (either group renders only if non-empty)
loading: implicit — StreamBuilder falls back to [] (snapshot.data ?? []) before first emission, no dedicated spinner
